refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (on-site vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (teleoperation, safety services, vehicle control) and anchors verification to measurable, time-bound operational data.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week). Log task completion (volume moved, tonnage), elapsed time, and safety incidents. Compare achieved productivity against productivity_target. Validate that all safety plan requirements were met during trial.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and accuracy.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote operator to command and monitor vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient for safe, productive task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and alert operator/site manager to unsafe conditions in real time.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The solution shall provide telemetry (productivity metrics, equipment state, safety status) to site manager for real-time and post-shift reporting.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate autonomous vehicle control (pre-programmed task sequences) with remote teleoperation fallback and real-time safety monitoring; measure productivity via onboard sensors and report via telemetry interface.

DP-B: Provide operator-in-the-loop teleoperation with high-fidelity video/sensor feedback, safety constraint enforcement at command level, and automated task templates to reduce cycle time; log all commands and outcomes for productivity analysis.

DP-C: Hybrid approach: autonomous task execution for routine cycles (dig, load, dump) with operator override capability; safety services continuously monitor vehicle state and worksite conditions; telemetry aggregates productivity and safety data for site manager dashboard.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Hybrid autonomous/teleoperated task execution with continuous safety monitoring and integrated telemetry reporting. Rationale: Balances productivity (autonomous routine cycles reduce operator fatigue and cycle time variance) with safety (operator override and real-time safety monitoring) and stakeholder needs (site manager visibility via telemetry). Aligns with high safety criticality constraint.

potential_sources: Vehicle control logic must be decoupled from safety services (safety must be able to override/halt vehicle independently).
Teleoperation command interface must not bypass safety constraint checks.
Telemetry reporting must not introduce latency that degrades real-time safety alerting.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C exhibits acceptable coupling if safety services are designed as an independent, higher-priority control layer. Teleoperation and vehicle control are logically coupled (operator commands vehicle), but this is inherent to the requirement and manageable via clear interface contracts.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Autonomous task sequences can be reliably pre-programmed for representative worksite conditions (soil type, equipment configuration, task geometry).
Assumption: Teleoperation latency (network + processing) can be kept ≤ 200 ms for safe operator control.
Risk: If autonomous sequences fail or worksite conditions deviate significantly, operator override may not be fast enough to prevent safety incident or productivity loss.
Risk: Hybrid control logic increases software complexity and testing burden; safety-critical code review required.
Risk: Telemetry data quality and timeliness depend on network reliability; loss of connectivity may blind site manager to safety/productivity status.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to explicitly specify: (1) autonomous task library scope and fallback triggers, (2) safety services architecture (independent watchdog, priority levels), (3) teleoperation latency budget and network redundancy strategy, (4) telemetry data freshness requirements. These details will inform IM assignment and lower-level decomposition.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration boundary encompassing vehicle control (autonomous + teleoperated), teleoperation subsystem, safety services, and telemetry aggregation. Owns the end-to-end productivity KPI delivery and safety compliance.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Site manager or pre-programmed task scheduler provides task definitions (dig location, load target, dump zone) to NGHE Solution. Tasking interface carries task geometry, priority, and safety constraints.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution reports productivity metrics (cycle time, volume moved, tonnage), vehicle state (position, fuel, wear), and safety status (alerts, constraint violations) to site manager dashboard and logging system.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution broadcasts real-time safety alerts (obstacle detected, operator override triggered, safety constraint violated) to site manager and safety manager for immediate action.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution communicates with remote teleoperation station and site manager via network link (5G, satellite, or hybrid). Carries teleoperation commands, telemetry, and safety alerts with latency and reliability requirements.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary and is the natural owner of the productivity KPI requirement. All sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are logically contained. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary.

Vehicle owns task execution (dig, load, dump) and onboard sensors, but does not own teleoperation command processing, safety services logic, or telemetry aggregation. If assigned here, would require Vehicle to expose interfaces for external teleoperation and safety control, creating diffusion and tight coupling.

Teleoperation owns remote operator interface and command transmission, but does not own vehicle control logic, safety services, or productivity measurement. Assigning productivity KPI here would create high diffusion (Teleoperation would need to coordinate with Vehicle and Safety Services for every task cycle).

Safety Services owns safety constraint enforcement and alerting, but does not own task execution, teleoperation, or productivity measurement. Assigning productivity KPI here would be a category error (safety is a constraint, not a productivity driver).

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary for CR-NGHE-001 because: (1) it is the top-level system that integrates all sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services), (2) productivity KPI is a system-level outcome that depends on coordinated execution across all sub-components, (3) external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are all system-level concerns, (4) ownership at this level enables clear allocation of sub-requirements to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services in the next decomposition level.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution is a high-level integration boundary; assigning the requirement here does not immediately clarify which sub-component is responsible for specific aspects (e.g., cycle time optimization vs. safety constraint enforcement). Mitigation: decompose into sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) and assign each to appropriate sub-module in next level.

Risk: If Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services are not yet fully defined as modules, ownership at NGHE Solution level may be premature. Mitigation: confirm that these modules exist and have clear interfaces before proceeding.

Risk: Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces depend on Network Link reliability; if network is unavailable, site manager loses visibility and safety alerting may be delayed. Mitigation: specify network redundancy and fallback alerting mechanisms in DP refinement.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules are defined with clear ownership and interfaces. In the next decomposition level, allocate sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) to these modules and refine their DPs and IMs. Ensure that Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces have latency and reliability budgets that support real-time safety and productivity reporting.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid autonomous/teleoperated task execution with continuous safety monitoring and integrated telemetry) directly addresses the refined FR (enable operator to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity ≥ target while maintaining safety compliance). DP-C is feasible, aligns with high safety criticality, and provides clear mechanisms for productivity measurement (telemetry) and safety enforcement (safety services). No decoupling issues identified.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Teleoperation command processing and vehicle control logic must be tightly coordinated; if split across multiple modules without clear interface contracts, command latency and safety constraint enforcement may be compromised. Mitigation: define Tasking and command interfaces with explicit latency and priority levels.

Diffusion Risk 2: Telemetry aggregation requires data from Vehicle (position, fuel, cycle time), Teleoperation (operator commands, override events), and Safety Services (constraint violations, alerts). If these modules do not have standardized telemetry output interfaces, NGHE Solution will need to implement custom data collection logic. Mitigation: define Telemetry interface schema and require all sub-modules to conform.

Diffusion Risk 3: SafetyAlert interface must be independent of Telemetry to ensure real-time alerting is not blocked by telemetry processing delays. Mitigation: implement SafetyAlert as a separate, higher-priority communication channel.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary for the top-level productivity KPI requirement. Containment is high (95%) because all sub-components (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are logically contained within NGHE Solution. Diffusion is low (5%) because external interfaces are well-defined and limited to Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Network Link. No evidence of module boundary violations or ownership ambiguity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-001 is feasible via representative operations trial: (1) conduct earthmoving tasks over agreed period, (2) log task completion (volume, tonnage) and elapsed time via Telemetry interface, (3) compare achieved productivity against productivity_target, (4) validate safety compliance via SafetyAlert logs and site manager reports. Verification does not require specialized test equipment beyond standard telemetry and logging infrastructure.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of CR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution is clear and unambiguous. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry subsystems respectively in the next decomposition level. Each sub-FR has a clear owner and verification method.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Refine DP-C to specify autonomous task library scope (e.g., standard dig-load-dump cycle, terrain constraints, equipment configurations) and fallback triggers (e.g., if autonomous cycle fails 3 times, revert to full teleoperation).
Revision 2: Define teleoperation latency budget (e.g., ≤ 200 ms end-to-end) and network redundancy strategy (e.g., primary 5G + fallback satellite link) to support safe operator control.
Revision 3: Specify Telemetry data freshness requirements (e.g., productivity metrics updated every 10 seconds, safety status updated every 1 second) to ensure site manager has timely visibility.
Revision 4: Confirm that Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules are defined with clear ownership, interfaces, and acceptance criteria before proceeding to next decomposition level.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level, non-atomic KPI requirement that requires decomposition into lower-level functional requirements (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) before detailed design can proceed. The refined FR and selected DP are sound and pass all gates, but the IM assignment (NGHE Solution) is a top-level integration boundary that does not directly map to implementable modules. Decomposition is necessary to allocate sub-FRs to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry subsystems and refine their DPs and IMs.
decision.justification_detail: The FR-DP-IM mapping is logically correct at the system level, but the NGHE Solution boundary is too broad to guide detailed design. The next level of decomposition will assign FR-NGHE-001.1 (vehicle task execution) to Vehicle module, FR-NGHE-001.2 (teleoperation) to Teleoperation module, FR-NGHE-001.3 (safety services) to Safety Services module, and FR-NGHE-001.4 (telemetry reporting) to a Telemetry/Logging subsystem. This will enable clear ownership, interface contracts, and verification methods for each sub-requirement.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and accuracy sufficient to achieve productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote operator to command and monitor vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient for safe, productive task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and alert operator/site manager to unsafe conditions in real time.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The solution shall provide telemetry (productivity metrics, equipment state, safety status) to site manager for real-time and post-shift reporting.

assumptions: Assumption 1: Autonomous task sequences can be reliably pre-programmed for representative worksite conditions (soil type, equipment configuration, task geometry).
Assumption 2: Teleoperation latency (network + processing) can be kept ≤ 200 ms for safe operator control.
Assumption 3: Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules are defined with clear ownership and interfaces.
Assumption 4: Network link (5G, satellite, or hybrid) is available and meets latency/reliability requirements for teleoperation and telemetry.
Assumption 5: Site manager and safety manager have access to telemetry and safety alert interfaces for real-time monitoring and post-shift reporting.
open_risks: Risk 1: If autonomous sequences fail or worksite conditions deviate significantly, operator override may not be fast enough to prevent safety incident or productivity loss.
Risk 2: Hybrid control logic increases software complexity and testing burden; safety-critical code review required.
Risk 3: Telemetry data quality and timeliness depend on network reliability; loss of connectivity may blind site manager to safety/productivity status.

Risk 4: Teleoperation command processing and vehicle control logic must be tightly coordinated; if split across multiple modules without clear interface contracts, command latency and safety constraint enforcement may be compromised.
Risk 5: SafetyAlert interface must be independent of Telemetry to ensure real-time alerting is not blocked by telemetry processing delays.
evidence_needed: Evidence 1: Autonomous task library definition and validation against representative worksite conditions.
Evidence 2: Teleoperation latency measurements (network + processing) under representative network conditions (5G, satellite, hybrid).
Evidence 3: Safety services architecture design and safety-critical code review (IEC 61508 or equivalent).
Evidence 4: Telemetry interface schema and data freshness requirements (e.g., productivity metrics every 10 seconds, safety status every 1 second).
Evidence 5: Network redundancy and fallback alerting mechanisms design and testing.
Evidence 6: Representative operations trial plan and acceptance criteria (productivity_target, safety compliance, operator workload).
